content.problemStatement:
You are given 222 integers AAA and BBB.

Find any integer XXX (0‚Č§X<2300 \leq X \lt 2^{30}0‚Č§X<230) such that ‚ą£(A‚äēX)‚ąí(B‚äēX)‚ą£| (A \oplus X) - (B \oplus X) |‚ą£(A‚äēX)‚ąí(B‚äēX)‚ą£ is **minimized**.

Here, ‚äē\oplus‚äē denotes the bitwise XOR operation, and ‚ą£A‚ą£|A|‚ą£A‚ą£ denotes the absolute value of AAA.

If there are multiple possible XXX that minimize the given expression, you may print any of them.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case will contain two space-separated integers AAA and BBB.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the integer XXX (0‚Č§X<2300 \leq X \lt 2^{30}0‚Č§X<230) that minimizes the given expression.

If there are multiple possible answers, you may print any of them.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: ‚ą£(4‚äē1)‚ąí(4‚äē2)‚ą£=‚ą£5‚ąí6‚ą£=1| (4 \oplus 1) - (4 \oplus 2) | = | 5 - 6 | = 1 ‚ą£(4‚äē1)‚ąí(4‚äē2)‚ą£=‚ą£5‚ąí6‚ą£=1.
It can be shown that difference cannot become less than 111.
Note that there are multiple possible answers here: for example X=0X = 0X=0 or X=3X = 3X=3 would also be acceptable.
Test case 222: ‚ą£(3‚äē0)‚ąí(3‚äē0)‚ą£=‚ą£3‚ąí3‚ą£=0| (3 \oplus 0) - (3 \oplus 0) | = | 3 - 3 | = 0 ‚ą£(3‚äē0)‚ąí(3‚äē0)‚ą£=‚ą£3‚ąí3‚ą£=0, which is the minimum possible difference.
Test case 333: ‚ą£(5‚äē11)‚ąí(1‚äē11)‚ą£=‚ą£14‚ąí10‚ą£=4| (5 \oplus 11) - (1 \oplus 11) | = | 14 - 10 | = 4 ‚ą£(5‚äē11)‚ąí(1‚äē11)‚ą£=‚ą£14‚ąí10‚ą£=4, which is the best we can do.
Test case 444: ‚ą£(0‚äē8)‚ąí(15‚äē8)‚ą£=‚ą£8‚ąí7‚ą£=1| (0 \oplus 8) - (15\oplus 8) | = |8 - 7| = 1‚ą£(0‚äē8)‚ąí(15‚äē8)‚ą£=‚ą£8‚ąí7‚ą£=1.
